name: Alto de Covelo and Alto da Portela Loop
name_original: Barco de Valdeorras a Alto de Covelo Vuelta
description: This difficult 53.5-mile road cycling loop in Pena Trevinca offers 5452 feet of elevation gain, passing the scenic Prada Reservoir.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around O Barco De Valdeorras traverse a landscape characterized by river valleys, significant elevation changes, and historic infrastructure. The region features numerous climbs and descents, offering varied terrain for road cyclists. This area is defined by its mountainous backdrop and the presence of rivers like the Sil, which shape the available routes. Best road cycling routes around O Barco De Valdeorras The most popular road cycling route is Alto de Covelo and Alto da Portela Loop, a 53.5 miles (86.1 km) trail that takes 4 hours 34 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is O Bañadoiro Suspension Bridge – Ponte Bibei loop from O Barco de Valdeorras, a difficult 48.4 miles (77.9 km) path. This route includes river crossings and challenging climbs. Local road cyclists also love the Sobradelo Medieval Bridge – Puente de Domingo Flórez loop from O Barco de Valdeorras, a 43.4 miles (69.8 km) trail leading through historic bridges and river valleys, often completed in about 3 hours 41 minutes. Road cycling around O Barco De Valdeorras is defined by river valleys, historic bridges, and challenging climbs. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate rides to difficult ascents. The routes in O Barco De Valdeorras are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore O Barco De Valdeorras's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around O Barco De Valdeorras?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes documented on komoot for the O Barco De Valdeorras region, offering a variety of distances and difficulty levels.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in O Barco De Valdeorras?
A: While many routes in O Barco De Valdeorras feature significant elevation changes, there is at least one easy route available. For a moderate option with less extreme climbing, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40015854" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valencia do Sil Dam loop from O Barco de Valdeorras&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a scenic ride with manageable ascents.

Q: What are the most challenging road cycling routes in the area?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e934007738/alto-de-covelo-and-alto-da-portela-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Covelo and Alto da Portela Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14936243" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Torrenovaes – O Bañadoiro Suspension Bridge loop from O Barco de Valdeorras&lt;/a&gt; offer substantial elevation gain and demanding climbs through mountainous terrain.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on road cycling routes in O Barco De Valdeorras?
A: The routes in O Barco De Valdeorras are characterized by river valleys, historic bridges, and a mountainous backdrop. You'll encounter picturesque views along rivers like the Sil, and pass through charming villages and natural landscapes.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or attractions along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, several routes pass by interesting landmarks. You can cycle past the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2969970" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;O Bañadoiro Suspension Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, or explore areas near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6322069" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pumares Village on the Camino de Invierno&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features historic sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3999102" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pazo de Arnado&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in O Barco De Valdeorras?
A: The best time for road cycling in O Barco De Valdeorras is typically during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder and the weather is generally more stable. Summer can be warm, especially in the valleys, while winter may bring colder temperatures and a higher chance of rain or snow in higher elevations.

Q: Are most road cycling routes in O Barco De Valdeorras circular or point-to-point?
A: Many of the popular road cycling routes around O Barco De Valdeorras are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31848610" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;O Bañadoiro Suspension Bridge – Ponte Bibei loop from O Barco de Valdeorras&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38168976" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sobradelo Medieval Bridge – Puente de Domingo Flórez loop from O Barco de Valdeorras&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in O Barco De Valdeorras?
A: The road cycling routes in O Barco De Valdeorras are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, challenging climbs, and scenic views along the river valleys and through historic areas.

Q: Are there options for long-distance road cycling tours?
A: Yes, the region offers several long-distance options. Routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14936243" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Torrenovaes – O Bañadoiro Suspension Bridge loop from O Barco de Valdeorras&lt;/a&gt; extend over 100 kilometers, providing a full day's ride with significant elevation changes.

Q: Is it possible to find refreshments or cafes along the routes?
A: While specific cafes are not listed for every route, O Barco De Valdeorras and the smaller villages you pass through often have local establishments where you can find refreshments. It's always advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on longer or more remote sections.

Q: What should I know about road conditions in the region?
A: Road cycling routes in O Barco De Valdeorras utilize a mix of paved roads. Given the mountainous and rural nature of the region, some roads may be narrower or have varying surface quality. Always be mindful of local traffic and road conditions, especially on descents.
tips: Ascent of 8km gaining 700m of difference in level with an average slope of 8.8%. The hard climb with spectacular views of the Sil Canyon. Go at your own pace!
View of Alto de Covelo towards Sill Canyon. Hold on tight. It's all downhill from here

faqs.question: Is the Alto de Covelo and Alto da Portela Loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as difficult. With a distance of over 86 km and more than 1600 meters of elevation gain, it presents a significant challenge best suited for experienced road cyclists with good physical conditioning.

faqs.question: What kind of bike is recommended for this route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's classification as a 'racebike' tour and the significant climbs involved, a well-maintained road bike is highly recommended. Its efficiency on paved surfaces will be crucial for tackling the long distances and elevation.

faqs.question: Where does the Alto de Covelo and Alto da Portela Loop start?
faqs.answer: The Komoot tour for this loop begins and ends near Barco de Valdeorras. Specific parking details would depend on your exact starting point within that area, but you'll generally find parking available in towns and villages along the route.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this road cycling loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain primarily consists of paved roads suitable for road cycling. You'll encounter sustained climbs, particularly on the ascents to Alto de Covelo and Alto da Portela, which feature varying gradients. Expect undulating landscapes typical of mountainous regions in Galicia and northern Portugal.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights on this loop include the challenging mountain passes of Alto de Covelo and Alto da Portela. You'll also pass by the scenic Prada Reservoir, offering beautiful views.

faqs.question: What natural features can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The route traverses diverse landscapes, including green, undulating hills, forests, and river valleys. As parts of the route are within the Pena Trevinca Natural Park area, you can expect pristine natural environments and expansive views typical of high-altitude regions.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this loop?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this loop is typically during the late spring, summer, and early autumn months (May to October). During these periods, the weather is generally more stable, with less rain and more favorable temperatures for long-distance cycling. Avoid winter due to potential snow and ice on higher passes.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Alto de Covelo and Alto da Portela Loop?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, so dogs are generally not suitable companions for the ride itself. If you are traveling with a dog, they would need to be secured or left at your accommodation. Always check local regulations if you plan to stop at any specific natural areas with your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route, especially in Pena Trevinca?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public roads through the Pena Trevinca Natural Park or the surrounding regions. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning protected areas, and be aware of any specific rules if you venture off the main roads into natural reserves.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. Notably, it covers significant portions of the [CIMA OU09] Alto de Covelo / Pico de San Bernabé * Freixido (24%) and [CIMA OU11] A Portela - A Moeda (17%). You'll also briefly cross parts of the Camiño de Inverno (12%) and other smaller local trails.

faqs.question: Are there places to find food and water along the route?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through some villages and towns, especially near the start/end point, services can be sparse in the more remote mountainous sections. It's highly advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks for the entire duration of the ride, especially given its length and difficulty. Plan your stops in advance.
